content:
The user wants me to extract the table from the provided image.

**1. Analyze the image:**
- The image is a technical specification sheet for a "Chip Resistor Surface Mount".
- It contains a large table with columns for "TEST", "TEST METHOD", "PROCEDURE", and "REQUIREMENTS".
- The table is split into two main parts visually, but it's one continuous table structure.
- There are sub-columns under "REQUIREMENTS" for the "Noise" test.

**2. Structure the table:**
- **Header:**
    - YAGEO logo
    - Product specification
    - Chip Resistor Surface Mount | RC | SERIES | 0805 (Pb Free)
    - Page number 8/10

- **Table Columns:**
    - TEST
    - TEST METHOD
    - PROCEDURE
    - REQUIREMENTS (This column has a nested structure for the "Noise" row)

- **Rows:**
    1.  **Solderability:**
        - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 208A; IEC 60115-1 4.17
        - Procedure: Solder bath at 245±3 °C; Dipping time: 2±0.5 seconds
        - Requirements: Well tinned (≥95% covered); No visible damage

    2.  **Bending Strength:**
        - Test Method: JIS C 5202.6.14; IEC 60115-1 4.15
        - Procedure: Resistors mounted on a 90 mm glass epoxy resin PCB (FR4); Bending: 5 mm
        - Requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol.; No visible damage

    3.  **Resistance to Solvent:**
        - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 215; IEC 60115-1 4.29
        - Procedure: Isopropylalcohol (C₃H₇OH) or dichloromethane (CH₂Cl₂) followed by brushing
        - Requirements: No smeared

    4.  **Noise:**
        - Test Method: JIS C 5202 5.9; IEC 60115-1 4.12
        - Procedure: Maximum voltage (Vᵣₘₛ) applied.
        - Requirements: This section is split into "Resistors range" and "Value".
            - R < 100 Ω: 10 dB
            - 100 Ω ≤ R < 1 KΩ: 20 dB
            - 1 KΩ ≤ R < 10 KΩ: 30 dB
            - 10 KΩ ≤ R < 100 KΩ: 40 dB
            - 100 KΩ ≤ R < 1 MΩ: 46 dB
            - 1 MΩ ≤ R ≤ 22 MΩ: 48 dB

    5.  **Humidity (steady state):**
        - Test Method: JIS C 5202 7.5; IEC 60115-8 4.24.8
        - Procedure: 1,000 hours; 40±2 °C; 93(+2/-3)% RH; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off
        - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol.

    6.  **Leaching:**
        - Test Method: EIA/IS 4.13B; IEC 60115-8 4.18
        - Procedure: Solder bath at 260±5 °C; Dipping time: 30±1 seconds
        - Requirements: No visible damage

    7.  **Intermittent Overload:**
        - Test Method: JIS C 5202 5.8
        - Procedure: At room temperature; 2.5 × RCWV applied for 1 second on and 25 seconds off; total 10,000 cycles
        - Requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol.

    8.  **Resistance to Vibration:**
        - Test Method: On request
        - Procedure: On request
        - Requirements: (Empty)

    9.  **Moisture Resistance Heat:**
        - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 106F; IEC 60115-1 4.24.2
        - Procedure: 42 cycles; total 1,000 hours; Shown as figure 9
        - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(2.0%+0.05Ω) for 5% tol.; No visible damage

**3. Formatting:**
- Use Markdown for the table.
- Handle the nested table for "Noise" requirements carefully.

**4. Footer:**
- Icons
- Date: Sep 03, 2004 V.2
- Website: www.yageo.com

Let's construct the Markdown.
```markdown
YAGEO Product specification 8
Chip Resistor Surface Mount RC SERIES 0805 (Pb Free) 10

| TEST | TEST METHOD | PROCEDURE | REQUIREMENTS |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Solderability | MIL-STD-202F-method 208A; IEC 60115-1 4.17 | Solder bath at 245±3 °C Dipping time: 2±0.5 seconds | Well tinned (≥95% covered) No visible damage |
| Bending Strength | JIS C 5202.6.14; IEC 60115-1 4.15 | Resistors mounted on a 90 mm glass epoxy resin PCB (FR4) Bending: 5 mm | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage |
| Resistance to Solvent | MIL-STD-202F-method 215; IEC 60115-1 4.29 | Isopropylalcohol (C₃H₇OH) or dichloromethane (CH₂Cl₂) followed by brushing | No smeared |
| Noise | JIS C 5202 5.9; IEC 60115-1 4.12 | Maximum voltage (Vᵣₘₛ) applied. | Resistors range Value |
| | | | R < 100 Ω 10 dB |
| | | | 100 Ω ≤ R < 1 KΩ 20 dB |
| | | | 1 KΩ ≤ R < 10 KΩ 30 dB |
| | | | 10 KΩ ≤ R < 100 KΩ 40 dB |
| | | | 100 KΩ ≤ R < 1 MΩ 46 dB |
| | | | 1 MΩ ≤ R ≤ 22 MΩ 48 dB |
| Humidity (steady state) | JIS C 5202 7.5; IEC 60115-8 4.24.8 | 1,000 hours; 40±2 °C; 93(+2/-3)% RH RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. |
| Leaching | EIA/IS 4.13B; IEC 60115-8 4.18 | Solder bath at 260±5 °C Dipping time: 30±1 seconds | No visible damage |
| Intermittent Overload | JIS C 5202 5.8 | At room temperature; 2.5 × RCWV applied for 1 second on and 25 seconds off; total 10,000 cycles | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. |
| Resistance to Vibration | On request | On request | |
| Moisture Resistance Heat | MIL-STD-202F-method 106F; IEC 60115-1 4.24.2 | 42 cycles; total 1,000 hours Shown as figure 9 | ±(0.5%+0.05Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage |

Sep 03, 2004 V.2 www.yageo.com
```
Wait, the "Noise" section is a bit tricky. It's not a separate row for each value, but rather a list of values corresponding to ranges. I will represent it as a nested table or just list the values in the cell. A nested table is cleaner.

Revised plan for Noise row:
| Noise | JIS C 5202 5.9; IEC 60115-1 4.12 | Maximum voltage (Vᵣₘₛ) applied. |
| --- | --- | --- |
| | | Resistors range | Value |
| | | R < 100 Ω | 10 dB |
| | | 100 Ω ≤ R < 1 KΩ | 20 dB |
| | | 1 KΩ ≤ R < 10 KΩ | 30 dB |
| | | 10 KΩ ≤ R < 100 KΩ | 40 dB |
| | | 100 KΩ ≤ R < 1 MΩ | 46 dB |
| | | 1 MΩ ≤ R ≤ 22 MΩ | 48 dB |

Actually, looking at the image, the "Resistors range" and "Value" headers are part of the "REQUIREMENTS" column. So the main table structure is:
TEST | TEST METHOD | PROCEDURE | REQUIREMENTS (which contains a sub-table for Noise)

Let's refine the Markdown to match the visual layout as closely as possible.

Final check of the text:
- "Product specification" is at the top right.
- "8" and "10" are in a box, likely page number.
- "Chip Resistor Surface Mount" is the title.
- "RC", "SERIES", "0805 (Pb Free)" are headers for the table below.

The table starts with headers: TEST, TEST METHOD, PROCEDURE, REQUIREMENTS.

Row 1: Solderability
Row 2: Bending Strength
Row 3: Resistance to Solvent
Row 4: Noise (with sub-rows for ranges)
Row 5: Humidity (steady state)
Row 6: Leaching
Row 7: Intermittent Overload
Row 8: Resistance to Vibration
Row 9: Moisture Resistance Heat

The "Noise" section is the most complex.
The "REQUIREMENTS" cell for Noise contains:
Header: Resistors range | Value
Row 1: R < 100 Ω | 10 dB
Row 2: 100 Ω ≤ R < 1 KΩ | 20 dB
Row 3: 1 KΩ ≤ R < 10 KΩ | 30 dB
Row 4: 10 KΩ ≤ R < 100 KΩ | 40 dB
Row 5: 100 KΩ ≤ R < 1 MΩ | 46 dB
Row 6: 1 MΩ ≤ R ≤ 22 MΩ | 48 dB

I will use a nested table for this part.

One detail: "Isopropylalcohol (C₃H₇OH)" - the subscript is important.
"Vᵣₘₛ" - subscript is important.
"CH₂Cl₂" - subscripts important.

Let's write the final output.
```markdown
YAGEO
Chip Resistor Surface Mount RC SERIES 0805 (Pb Free)
Product specification 8 10

| TEST | TEST METHOD | PROCEDURE | REQUIREMENTS |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Solderability | MIL-STD-202F-method 208A; IEC 60115-1 4.17 | Solder bath at 245±3 °C Dipping time: 2±0.5 seconds | Well tinned (≥95% covered) No visible damage |
| Bending Strength | JIS C 5202.6.14; IEC 60115-1 4.15 | Resistors mounted on a 90 mm glass epoxy resin PCB (FR4) Bending: 5 mm | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage |
| Resistance to Solvent | MIL-STD-202F-method 215; IEC 60115-1 4.29 | Isopropylalcohol (C₃H₇OH) or dichloromethane (CH₂Cl₂) followed by brushing | No smeared |
| Noise | JIS C 5202 5.9; IEC 60115-1 4.12 | Maximum voltage (Vᵣₘₛ) applied. | Resistors range Value |
| | | | R < 100 Ω 10 dB |
| | | | 100 Ω ≤ R < 1 KΩ 20 dB |
| | | | 1 KΩ ≤ R < 10 KΩ 30 dB |
| | | | 10 KΩ ≤ R < 100 KΩ 40 dB |
| | | | 100 KΩ ≤ R < 1 MΩ 46 dB |
| | | | 1 MΩ ≤ R ≤ 22 MΩ 48 dB |
| Humidity (steady state) | JIS C 5202 7.5; IEC 60115-8 4.24.8 | 1,000 hours; 40±2 °C; 93(+2/-3)% RH RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. |
| Leaching | EIA/IS 4.13B; IEC 60115-8 4.18 | Solder bath at 260±5 °C Dipping time: 30±1 seconds | No visible damage |
| Intermittent Overload | JIS C 5202 5.8 | At room temperature; 2.5 × RCWV applied for 1 second on and 25 seconds off; total 10,000 cycles | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. |
| Resistance to Vibration | On request | On request | |
| Moisture Resistance Heat | MIL-STD-202F-method 106F; IEC 60115-1 4.24.2 | 42 cycles; total 1,000 hours Shown as figure 9 | ±(0.5%+0.05Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage |

Sep 03, 2004 V.2 www.yageo.com
```
The nested table for Noise is a bit awkward in standard markdown. I'll try to format it as a single cell with line breaks or a separate small table if possible. Since I can't render a nested table perfectly in all markdown viewers, I will use a single cell with line breaks for the ranges and values.

Actually, looking at the image, the "Resistors range" and "Value" headers are aligned with the rows below them. It looks like a sub-table. I will represent it as a sub-table within the cell.

Wait, the "Noise" row spans multiple rows in the visual representation because of the sub-values.
So, the main table has:
- Row 1: Solderability
- Row 2: Bending Strength
- Row 3: Resistance to Solvent
- Row 4: Noise (This row is tall because of the sub-values)
- Row 5: Humidity
- Row 6: Leaching
- Row 7: Intermittent Overload
- Row 8: Resistance to Vibration
- Row 9: Moisture Resistance Heat

I will use a nested table for the Noise requirements to keep it organized.

One final check on the "Moisture Resistance Heat" row.
Test Method: MIL-STD
